message:
bd-nonv: unit tests for profiles, rulesets, and pane bookmarks

37 new tests covering:
- config_profiles: name validation, canonicalization, manifest management (8)
- rulesets: name validation, PatternsConfigPatch merging, profile resolution (16)
- storage: pane bookmark edge cases (aliases, tags, nulls, case sensitivity) (13)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-lwan","title":"Metrics + diagnostics for capture throttling","description":"## What
Expose metrics and CLI diagnostics showing capture budgets and throttling decisions.

## Why
Without visibility, operators can't tune priorities safely.

## How
- Add counters for throttled captures, skipped panes
- Extend `wa status/health` to show budget state

- Metrics are exposed (Prometheus or JSON)
- Status output includes reasoned throttling info","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:09.174601153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:19:09.817261176Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:19:09.817138668Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lwan","depends_on_id":"bd-2ipp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-lwan","depends_on_id":"bd-396b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-lwan","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Wired SchedulerSnapshot through ObservationRuntime → HealthSnapshot.  {"id":"bd-mwst","title":"Document Lua removal: CHANGELOG, migration guide, architecture update","description":"## Overview

Update all documentation to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and the new polling-based architecture. This ensures users understand the change and can migrate smoothly.

## Documentation Updates Required

### 1. CHANGELOG.md

Add entry for the version that removes STATUS_UPDATE_LUA:

\`\`\`markdown
## [0.2.0] - 2026-XX-XX

### Changed

- **BREAKING**: Removed Lua-based status update hook (\`update-status\`)
  - Dramatically improves WezTerm performance by eliminating high-frequency Lua callbacks
  - Pane metadata now obtained via polling (\`wezterm cli list\`)
  - Alt-screen detection now via escape sequence parsing (more reliable)

### Removed

- \`wa event --from-status\` CLI command (internal, not public API)
- STATUS_UPDATE_LUA snippet from \`wa setup\` output

### Migration

If you previously ran \`wa setup\`, run it again to update your wezterm.lua:

\`\`\`bash
wa setup --wezterm
\`\`\`

This will automatically remove the deprecated Lua code from the WA-managed block.
\`\`\`

### 2. README.md / AGENTS.md

Update any references to status updates:

- Remove mentions of "real-time status updates"
- Update architecture diagram if it shows Lua→wa flow
- Add note about polling-based pane metadata

### 3. PLAN.md / PLAN_CODEX.md

Update the specification:

**Section to update:**
- §2.7 (Status update specification) — mark as deprecated/removed
- Appendix E.1 — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA documentation

**Add new section:**
- §X.X Polling-based pane metadata
- Explain how alt-screen detection works via escape sequences

### 4. Architecture Documentation

Create or update architecture docs explaining:

1. **Before (Lua-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   WezTerm update-status event (60Hz)
     → Lua handler (runs every event)
       → Rate limit check
         → JSON serialization
           → wezterm.background_child_process
             → wa event --from-status
               → IPC to watcher
   \`\`\`

2. **After (Polling-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   wa watcher ingest loop (configurable interval)
     → wezterm cli list --format json
       → Parse pane metadata
     → ScreenStateTracker.process_output()
       → Parse alt-screen escape sequences
   \`\`\`

### 5. Inline Code Documentation

Update doc comments in affected files:

- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs: Update module-level docs
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs: Document polling-based metadata
- crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs: Document escape sequence detection

### 6. Migration Guide

Create a dedicated migration section (can be in README or separate file):

\`\`\`markdown
## Migrating from wa 0.1.x to 0.2.x

### What Changed

In 0.2.0, we removed the Lua-based status update hook to dramatically improve WezTerm performance.

### Impact

- **No action required** for most users
- If you have custom integrations with \`wa event --from-status\`, see below

### Steps to Upgrade

1. **Update wa** to the latest version
2. **Re-run setup** to update your wezterm.lua:
   \`\`\`bash
   wa setup --wezterm
   \`\`\`
3. **Restart WezTerm** to pick up config changes

### Verifying the Migration

After upgrading, your wezterm.lua's WA-managed block should NOT contain:
- \`wezterm.on('update-status'\`
- \`wa_last_status_update\`
- \`WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS\`

It SHOULD still contain:
- \`wezterm.on('user-var-changed'\` (for agent signaling)

### Breaking Changes

- \`wa event --from-status\` command removed
- StatusUpdate IPC message type removed
- These were internal implementation details, not public APIs
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated with breaking change notice
- [ ] README.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] PLAN.md §2.7 marked as deprecated
- [ ] Architecture documentation updated
- [ ] Migration guide written
- [ ] Code doc comments updated
- [ ] All docs spell-checked and links verified

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-o2h4 (E2E tests passing, feature complete)

## Files to Modify/Create

- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md (if applicable)
- AGENTS.md (if applicable)
- PLAN.md
- PLAN_CODEX.md
- docs/migration-0.2.md (new, optional)
- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs (doc comments)
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs (doc comments)

## References

- Existing PLAN.md: project root

 {"id":"bd-nn9e","title":"Diff/preview + rollback semantics","description":"## What
Provide a safe diff/preview and rollback workflow for applying profiles.

## Why
Users must understand changes before switching to avoid surprises.

## How
- `wa config profile diff <name>` shows changes
- `wa config profile apply <name> --dry-run` shows preview
- Store last applied profile for rollback

## Success Criteria
- Preview output is stable and redacted
- Rollback restores previous config without loss","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltGlen","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:47.423121303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T09:03:16.943062435Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T09:03:16.942990832Z","close_reason":"Implemented config profile diff/apply/rollback with manifest updates and backup/rollback flow","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nn9e","depends_on_id":"bd-c5xl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-nn9e","depends_on_id":"bd-qsnh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-npuj","title":"Fix alt-screen override disabling text detection","description":"Stop forcing false alt-screen state from deprecated pane metadata; allow snapshot-based detection to drive gaps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:40:16.441462968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:40:36.097789246Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:40:36.097718545Z","close_reason":"Returned None when no authoritative status; avoids overriding text-based alt-screen detection. cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-o2h4","title":"Update E2E tests: remove status_update scenario, add alt-screen detection test","description":"## Overview

Update the E2E test suite to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and addition of alt-screen detection via escape sequences.

## Current E2E Test State

The E2E test harness (scripts/e2e_test.sh) has a "status_update" scenario that:
1. Writes a minimal wezterm.lua with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
2. Spawns a pane
3. Sends a status update via \`wa event --from-status\`
4. Verifies the watcher receives it

This scenario will become invalid and must be removed.

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove status_update Scenario

In SCENARIO_REGISTRY (~line 340):
\`\`\`bash
# REMOVE this entry:
"status_update:Validate status update forwarding lane (wezterm.lua -> wa event -> watcher)"
\`\`\`

Remove the entire \`run_scenario_status_update()\` function (~lines 2550-2650).

### 2. Add alt-screen Detection Scenario

New scenario: "alt_screen_detection"

Purpose: Verify wa detects alt-screen transitions via escape sequence parsing (not Lua).

Test flow:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    # 1. Start wa watch
    # 2. Spawn a pane
    # 3. Send ESC[?1049h (enter alt-screen) to pane
    # 4. Verify wa detects alt-screen = true
    # 5. Send ESC[?1049l (leave alt-screen)
    # 6. Verify wa detects alt-screen = false
}
\`\`\`

Implementation sketch:
\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    local scenario_dir="$1"
    local wait_timeout="${TEST_WAIT_TIMEOUT:-30}"
    
    log_step "Starting wa watch..."
    wa watch --foreground > "$scenario_dir/wa_watch.log" 2>&1 &
    local wa_pid=$!
    sleep 2
    
    log_step "Spawning test pane..."
    local pane_id=$(wezterm cli spawn --cwd /tmp -- bash)
    sleep 1
    
    log_step "Verifying initial state (not alt-screen)..."
    local state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    local is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false initially, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen enter sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 h
    printf '\e[?1049h' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5  # Allow detection
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen detected..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "true" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=true after ESC[?1049h, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen leave sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 l
    printf '\e[?1049l' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen exited..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false after ESC[?1049l, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Alt-screen detection via escape sequences works!"
    cleanup_pane "$pane_id"
    kill "$wa_pid" 2>/dev/null
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Update uservar_forwarding Scenario

Ensure the uservar_forwarding scenario still works (it should, we're only removing status updates).

Verify it doesn't depend on status update functionality.

### 4. Add Regression Test: No Lua Status Hook

New scenario: "no_lua_status_hook"

Purpose: Verify wezterm.lua doesn't contain STATUS_UPDATE_LUA after setup.

\`\`\`bash
run_scenario_no_lua_status_hook() {
    # 1. Run wa setup
    # 2. Read wezterm.lua
    # 3. Verify it contains USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
    # 4. Verify it does NOT contain 'update-status'
    # 5. Verify it does NOT contain 'wa_last_status_update'
}
\`\`\`

## Test Fixtures

Create/update test fixtures:

### New: tests/e2e/alt_screen_enter.txt
\`\`\`
ESC[?1049h
\`\`\`

### New: tests/e2e/alt_screen_leave.txt
\`\`\`
ESC[?1049l
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] status_update scenario removed from SCENARIO_REGISTRY
- [ ] run_scenario_status_update() function removed
- [ ] alt_screen_detection scenario added and passing
- [ ] no_lua_status_hook scenario added and passing
- [ ] uservar_forwarding scenario still passes
- [ ] All other E2E tests unaffected
- [ ] CI passes

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2z6d (CLI handling removed)
- Depends on: bd-2v4t (alt-screen detection implemented)

## Files to Modify

- scripts/e2e_test.sh:
  - Remove status_update from SCENARIO_REGISTRY
  - Remove run_scenario_status_update()
  - Add alt_screen_detection scenario
  - Add no_lua_status_hook scenario

## References

- E2E test harness: scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Scenario registry: ~line 335
- Alt-screen sequences: ESC[?1049h/l","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:46:29.380681973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:51:20.332602643Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:51:20.332509851Z","close_reason":"E2E status_update removal + alt_screen/no_lua scenarios already present; verified in scripts/e2e_test.sh","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2v4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-o2h4","depends_on_id":"bd-2z6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ybyi","title":"Cleanup engine + safe preview","description":"## What
Implement cleanup logic with dry-run preview.

## Why
Operators need to know exactly what will be deleted before confirming.

## How
- Dry-run mode lists counts per table
- Apply mode deletes in batches with progress
- Always requires explicit confirmation
- If schema changes are needed, route through wa-y6g migrations

 {"id":"bd-yt9x","title":"Design saved search model + scheduling semantics","description":"## What
Define the saved search schema (query, filters, scope), storage location, and scheduling semantics.

## Why
A clear model prevents CLI/TUI/web divergence and ensures alerts are deterministic.

## How
- Specify fields (name, query, pane scope, filters, cadence, enabled flag, last_run_at)
- Define schedule resolution (fixed interval, no cron yet)
- Define alert payload shape (redacted preview + counts)

## Risks
- Overlapping with existing search explainability; ensure reuse of core search APIs.

## Success Criteria
- Schema and scheduling contract documented in this bead
- Downstream tasks can implement without guesswork","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:20.194559968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:25.310043105Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:25.309960873Z","close_reason":"Draft spec in docs/saved-searches-spec.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yt9x","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
